title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: ares() fits Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) models, flexible nonparametric regression technique introduced Friedman (1991). forward pass adds basis functions form max(0, ±(x - knot)) one pair time, scaled parent term allow interactions. backward pass prunes terms minimising GCV criterion (K-fold CV criterion pmethod = "cv" nfold > 0). ares() mirrors API earth gaussian core adds: hyperparameter autotune, K-fold CV pruning, row-bootstrap bagging, observation weights, binomial / poisson / gamma GLM families selected basis, residual-variance prediction intervals.

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
code: library(roadrunner) fit <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, nthreads = 2) print(fit) #> Call: #> ares.formula(x = mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, nthreads = 2) #>  #> MARS fit:  family = gaussian  #>   Selected terms: 6 of 21 forward-pass terms #>   RSS: 91.81  #>   GCV: 6.662  #>   Degree: 1   Penalty: 2   nthreads: 2 head(predict(fit)) #> [1] 19.36172 20.45321 25.35910 19.52265 17.68805 19.22091 head(predict(fit, mtcars[1:5, ])) #> [1] 19.36172 20.45321 25.35910 19.52265 17.68805

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: two implementations agree numerically within fraction percent RSS even pick slightly different knot sets.
code:
if (requireNamespace("earth", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(20260509)   n <- 200; p <- 5   x <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p)   y <- 10 * sin(pi * x[, 1] * x[, 2]) + 20 * (x[, 3] - 0.5)^2 +        10 * x[, 4] + 5 * x[, 5] + rnorm(n)   fa <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 2)   fe <- earth::earth(x, y, degree = 2)   cat("ares  RSS:", round(fa$rss, 3), " GCV:", round(fa$gcv, 3),       " nterms:", length(fa$selected.terms), "
")   cat("earth RSS:", round(fe$rss, 3), " GCV:", round(fe$gcv, 3),       " nterms:", length(fe$selected.terms), "
")   cat("relative RSS difference:",       round(abs(fa$rss - fe$rss) / fe$rss, 4), "
") } #> ares  RSS: 222.61  GCV: 1.707  nterms: 16  #> earth RSS: 204.18  GCV: 1.518  nterms: 15  #> relative RSS difference: 0.0903

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: ares parallel-deterministic construction: changing nthreads change output bit--bit.
code: set.seed(42) n <- 150; p <- 5 x <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p) y <- 5 * pmax(0, x[, 1] - 0.5) * pmax(0, x[, 2] - 0.3) + rnorm(n) f1 <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 1) f2 <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 2) identical(f1$selected.terms, f2$selected.terms) #> [1] TRUE max(abs(f1$coefficients - f2$coefficients)) #> [1] 0

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: autotune = TRUE runs inner K-fold CV grid search (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) refits winner full data. warm-start phase fits small subsample first short-circuits best-per-degree gap decisive.
code: set.seed(7) fit_at <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, autotune = TRUE,                autotune.speed = "fast", seed.cv = 1L, nthreads = 2) fit_at$autotune$degree #> [1] 1 fit_at$autotune$penalty #> [1] 1 fit_at$autotune$nk #> [1] 42

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: Forward + backward run gaussian; selected basis refit stats::glm.fit(family = binomial()). predict() returns probabilities default, linear predictor via type = "link".
code: set.seed(1) n <- 400 x <- matrix(rnorm(n * 4), n, 4) p <- plogis(0.5 * x[, 1] + 1.2 * x[, 2] * (x[, 2] > 0) - 0.4 * x[, 3]) y <- rbinom(n, 1, p) fb <- ares(x, y, family = "binomial", degree = 2, nthreads = 2) range(predict(fb)) #> [1] 0.2443712 0.9915275

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: varmod = "const" varmod = "lm" (gaussian ) stores residual variance model fit time. predict(interval = "pint") returns matrix c("fit", "lwr", "upr") columns.

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: Fast, low-dependency machine learning algorithms R. roadrunner ships C++ backed implementations classical ML algorithms thin, base-R-style interfaces. first algorithm ares(), Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines fitter; follow.

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: Low dependency. Rcpp RcppParallel runtime. tidyverse, rlang, S4. Fast. C++ engines via Rcpp multi-core scoring via RcppParallel (TBB). Deterministic. Fits bit--bit identical across thread counts fixed seed. Familiar API. Base-R style: formula matrix interfaces, standard S3 methods (predict, print, summary, plot), argument names mirror existing R packages one exists.

title: Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
Fast, Low-Dependency Machine Learning Algorithms
text: See ares vignette autotune, classification, weights, bagging, prediction-interval examples.
code: library(roadrunner)  # Regression fit <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, degree = 2) predict(fit, head(mtcars))  # Classification y <- as.integer(mtcars$am) fitc <- ares(as.matrix(mtcars[, -9]), y, family = "binomial") predict(fitc)  # Hands-free hyperparameter tuning fitt <- ares(mpg ~ ., data = mtcars, autotune = TRUE,              autotune.speed = "fast", seed.cv = 1L) fitt$autotune$degree

what: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: Fits MARS model (Friedman 1991) using fast least-squares forward pass parallelized knot search backward subset-selection minimizes GCV criterion. implementation aims numerical parity earth::earth() gaussian-core taking advantage multi-core CPUs reduce wall-clock fitting time.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
code: ares(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'formula' ares(x, data = NULL, ..., y = NULL)  # Default S3 method ares(   x,   y,   degree = 1L,   nk = NULL,   penalty = NULL,   thresh = 0.001,   minspan = 0L,   endspan = 0L,   adjust.endspan = 1L,   auto.linpreds = FALSE,   fast.k = 10L,   fast.beta = 1,   nprune = NULL,   pmethod = c("backward", "none", "cv"),   nfold = 0L,   ncross = 1L,   stratify = TRUE,   seed.cv = NULL,   cv.1se = FALSE,   autotune = FALSE,   autotune.speed = c("balanced", "quality", "fast"),   autotune.warmstart = TRUE,   n.boot = 0L,   na.action = c("impute", "omit"),   family = c("gaussian", "binomial", "poisson", "gamma"),   weights = NULL,   varmod = c("none", "const", "lm"),   trace = 0L,   nthreads = 0L,   ... )

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: x numeric matrix data frame predictors, model formula calling formula method. ... Additional arguments. Currently ignored. data Used formula method . data frame. y numeric response vector. Length must equal nrow(x). degree Maximum interaction degree. Default 1. nk Maximum number basis terms forward pass. Default min(200, max(20, 2 * ncol(x))) + 1. penalty GCV penalty. Default (degree > 1) 3 else 2. thresh Forward-pass relative-RSS early-stop threshold. Default 0.001. minspan Minimum knot span. 0 (default) selects automatic value. endspan Knot offset data ends. 0 (default) selects automatic. adjust.endspan Multiplier applied endspan candidate hinge deepen existing interaction (parent term degree least 1). Default 1 gaussian / poisson / gamma, 2 binomial (auto-flipped v0.0.0.9026 /B-test justification auto.linpreds). Pass 1 2 explicitly override family default. auto.linpreds TRUE, best forward-pass knot candidate hinge sits boundary variable's eligible range, substitutes linear term (dirs = 2) hinge pair. Default FALSE gaussian / poisson / gamma, TRUE binomial (auto- flipped v0.0.0.9026 based inst/sims/v0.25 /B test: earth- like settings tie improve AUC mlbench binary cells, regress gaussian regression cells). Pass explicit value override family default. fast.k Fast-MARS priority cache size (Friedman 1993). forward pass always rescores pairs whose parent added previous step; top fast.k remaining stale pairs (ranked age-discounted cached score) also rescored, rest contribute cached reduction. 0 disables cache (every pair rescored every step). Default 10. fast.beta Age-penalty fast-MARS priority cache: stale pair's effective score cached_rss_red / (1 + fast.beta * age). Default 1.0. matters fast.k > 0. nprune Maximum number terms backward pruning (default nk). pmethod Pruning method: "backward" (default), "none", "cv" K-fold cross-validated subset selection. "cv" requires nfold > 0 (implicitly sets nfold = 5 specified). nfold Number CV folds (default 0 = CV; GCV-based backward selection). nfold > 0, pmethod promoted "cv". ncross Number CV repetitions nfold > 0. repetition builds fresh fold partition; per-size mean MSE averaged across nfold * ncross evaluations. Default 1. stratify TRUE (default), folds quantile-stratified y fold similar response distribution. Useful small n / skewed responses; cheap. seed.cv Optional integer seed fold partitioning. NULL (default) uses current RNG state; pass integer reproducible CV partitions. cv.1se TRUE, applies one-standard-error rule pmethod="cv": among sizes whose mean CV-MSE within one standard error minimum, pick smallest. Trades bit accuracy parsimony. Default FALSE (pick argmin mean CV-MSE, .e. size minimises holdout error directly). autotune TRUE, runs inner cross-validated grid search (degree, penalty) refits winner. grid degree {1, 2} (extended {1, 2, 3} nk large enough) crossed penalty {0.5*d, 1.0*d, 2.0*d, 3.0*d} degree d. Inner CV uses nfold (default 5 autotune nfold == 0) respects seed.cv. chosen (degree, penalty) full grid CV-MSE returned $autotune. Default FALSE (current grf-style fast default path). Subsequent versions extend grid (v0.16: nk, v0.17: autotune.speed, v0.18: n.boot). autotune.speed One "balanced" (default), "quality", "fast". meaningful autotune = TRUE. "quality": forces fast.k = 0 every cell (fast-MARS priority cache; every (parent, var) pair rescored every step). Matches v0.10 quality v0.10 wall-clock cost. "fast": forces fast.k = 5. aggressive cache; cheapest. "balanced" (default): sweeps fast.k {10, 25, Inf} (Inf means "cache") inside autotune grid picks smallest fast.k whose mean CV-MSE within 1% best. Trades marginal accuracy hit marginal speed gain. autotune.warmstart TRUE (default; meaningful autotune = TRUE) n >= 200, ares first runs autotune 15% subsample (capped 200 rows). subsample decisive winner – best-per-degree CV-MSE 5% next-best degree's best cell – (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) used directly refit full data, skipping full-data grid. Cuts autotune wall-clock ~5x well-separated DGPs. n.boot Number bootstrap replicate fits bagging. Default 0 (bagging). > 0, result holds list n.boot ares fits $boot$fits, fit row-bootstrap sample data. augmented predict() averages predictions across replicates plus central fit reports per-prediction standard deviation attr(predictions, "sd"). Bagging composes autotune – replicate fits central fit's chosen (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) cost (n.boot + 1) * fit_cost, autotune grid times n.boot. na.action Strategy missing values x. Either "impute" (default) "omit". "impute" replaces column's NAs column's median (computed non-missing rows) stores medians fit predict() can reapply future newdata. "omit" drops every row NA x. actions emit warning summarising affected rows / columns. Missing values y always hard error – sensible way impute regression target. NaN +/-Inf x also rejected outright regardless na.action. family Response family. One "gaussian" (default; numeric y, identity link, OLS coefficients), "binomial" (binary y, logit link), "poisson" (non-negative integer-valued y, log link), "gamma" (strictly positive y, log link). non-gaussian families forward backward passes still run original numeric y gaussian (matching earth\'s GLM strategy); backward pruning selected basis refit response via stats::glm.fit() appropriate family object, $coefficients, $fitted.values, $linear.predictor come GLM. Bagging, CV pruning, autotune, weights compose GLM families; inner model-selection still uses (weighted) Gaussian MSE latent scale (cheap adequate term selection — final coefficients use IRLS). weights Optional non-negative numeric vector length nrow(x) weighted least squares fitting. Default NULL (unweighted OLS). supplied, engine implements WLS via sqrt(weights) transform design response, forward + backward consume weights (knot selection, GCV pruning, CV pruning, autotune weight-aware). Weights renormalised internally mean(weights) = 1; keeps GCV denominator valid ("effective sample size" equals n). NA / NaN / negative weights rejected. weights = rep(1, n) fit byte-identical unweighted path. varmod Variance-model strategy used predict.ares() build prediction intervals. One : "none" (default): variance model stored. predict() returns conditional mean; interval = "pint" unavailable. "const": store single training residual standard deviation sigma_hat = sqrt(weighted_RSS / df_residual). PIs yhat +/- qt(0.975, df) * sigma_hat. "lm": fit small linear model |resid| ~ yhat training fit use prediction (multiplied sqrt(pi/2) estimates sigma) new yhat. Captures simple heteroscedasticity. meaningful family = "gaussian". binomial / poisson / gamma variance mean-determined; argument silently ignored (PIs require family-specific intervals implemented — see ?stats::predict.glm ). Defaults "none" (SEs default). trace Trace level. 0 = silent (default), 1 = forward-pass progress. nthreads Number threads. 0 (default) selects RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads(). CRAN-distributed examples bundled vignette cap 2.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: object class "ares" – list components coefficients, bx, dirs, cuts, selected.terms, rss, gcv, rss.per.subset, gcv.per.subset, fitted.values, residuals, namesx, call, plus echoed control parameters. family non-gaussian fit additionally carries $family, $glm (small list deviance, null.deviance, df.null, df.residual, aic, converged, iter, plus family-specific fields), $linear.predictor (length n), $fitted.values response scale: probabilities binomial (plogis(lp)), counts/rates poisson (exp(lp)), positive means gamma (exp(lp)).

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: Two interfaces provided. ares.default() accepts numeric predictor matrix x numeric response vector y. ares.formula() accepts model formula data frame.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares

what: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
text: Predicts response (linear predictor) new data fitted ares MARS model. bagged fits, returns mean across central fit bootstrap replicates; se.fit = TRUE, per-row bag standard deviation attached "sd" attribute. gaussian fits stored variance model (varmod = "const" "lm"), interval = "pint" returns prediction intervals.

title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
code: # S3 method for class 'ares' predict(   object,   newdata = NULL,   type = c("response", "link"),   se.fit = FALSE,   interval = c("none", "pint"),   level = 0.95,   ... )

title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
text: object object class "ares". newdata numeric matrix data frame new predictors. NULL (default), returns object$fitted.values. type Prediction scale. "response" (default) returns plogis(eta) binomial fits, exp(eta) poisson / gamma, fitted mean gaussian fits; "link" returns linear predictor eta. Ignored gaussian models (link response coincide). se.fit TRUE object fit n.boot > 0, returned vector carries "sd" attribute holding per-prediction bag standard deviation. Default FALSE (plain numeric vector). interval Type interval return. "none" (default) returns fitted mean. "pint" returns matrix columns c("fit", "lwr", "upr") using variance model stored fit time (varmod = "const" "lm"; gaussian ). interval = "pint" variance model stored, call errors informative message. level Confidence level prediction intervals interval = "pint". Default 0.95. ... Additional arguments. Currently unused.

title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
text: numeric vector length nrow(newdata), matrix c("fit", "lwr", "upr") columns interval = "pint". se.fit = TRUE bag fits present, result carries attr(., "sd") per-row sample SD across replicates.

title: Predictions from an ares fit — predict.ares
code: fit <- ares(as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]), mtcars$mpg, nthreads = 2) p <- predict(fit, as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]))

code: fit <- ares(as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]), mtcars$mpg, nthreads = 2) summary(fit) #> Call: #> ares.default(x = as.matrix(mtcars[, -1]), y = mtcars$mpg, nthreads = 2) #>  #> MARS summary:  family = gaussian  #>   Selected: 6 of 21 forward-pass terms #>   RSS = 91.81364   GCV = 6.662215   degree = 1  #>  #>           term        coef #>    (Intercept) 19.71062781 #>    h(145-disp)  0.08426401 #>      h(123-hp)  0.08435673 #>      h(gear-4)  3.85511175 #>  h(17.05-qsec) -2.45008107 #>     h(wt-2.78) -2.95313879
